# Fenrir Web Framework

Fenrir is a high-performance, modern hybrid Python web framework that combines Flask, FastAPI, Falcon, and Sanic paradigms into a single unified application environment, powered by Asteri.

## Core Documentation
- Introduction: /docs/introduction - Framework overview and architecture.
- Installation: /docs/installation - Package requirements and how to install.
- Project Structure: /docs/project-structure - Recommended file and directory layouts.
- Quick Start Guide: /docs/quick-start - Hello World walkthrough.
- Basic Concepts: /docs/basic-concepts - Framework foundations.
- Routing System: /docs/routing - How routing, path params, and regex works.
- Request & Response: /docs/request-response - Accessing headers, variables, custom responses.
- Dependency Injection: /docs/dependency-injection - Using Depends decorator.
- Data Validation: /docs/data-validation - Pydantic models integration.
- Context Locals: /docs/context-locals - Request, session, and global context variables.
- Class-Based Resources: /docs/class-based-resources - Falcon-style class resources.
- File Upload: /docs/file-upload - Standard and multipart file uploads.
- WebSocket Support: /docs/websocket - Real-time websocket endpoints.
- Server-Sent Events (SSE): /docs/server-sent-events - Streaming real-time notification events.
- Jinja2 Templating: /docs/templating - Rendering HTML templates.
- Error Handling: /docs/error-handling - Handling status codes and custom exceptions.
- Error Handling Compatibility: /docs/error-handling-compatibility - Multi-style error handling compatibility.
- Middleware: /docs/middleware - Request and response middleware hooks.
- Middleware Classes: /docs/middleware-classes - CORS, GZip, RequestID, RateLimit, BodyLimit, CSRF middleware.
- Sessions: /docs/sessions - Secure cookie, in-memory, and Redis session backends.
- Pagination: /docs/pagination - Paginated list responses.
- Background Tasks: /docs/background-tasks - Running background workers.
- Authentication & Security: /docs/authentication-security - API keys, JWT, Bearer tokens, OAuth2, OpenID Connect.
- Blueprints: /docs/blueprints - Routing modularization.
- Configuration: /docs/configuration - App settings and environment variables.
- Testing Guide: /docs/testing - Writing test cases with pytest and TestClient.
- CLI Tools Reference: /docs/cli-tools - Run, inspect, and benchmark commands.
- Advanced Features: /docs/advanced-features - WSGI mounting, compat modes, multiple response models.
- Signals System: /docs/signals - Event-driven programming with signals.
- JSON Provider: /docs/json-provider - Custom JSON serialization and tagged types.
- OpenAPI Customization: /docs/openapi-customization - Swagger UI, ReDoc, route metadata.
- Best Practices: /docs/best-practices - Performance, security, type hints.
- Conclusion: /docs/conclusion - Closing notes and changelog.

## Technical Specifications
- Support for FastAPI-style decorators and Pydantic validation.
- Flask-style request/session contexts and Jinja2 rendering.
- Falcon-style class-based resources and before/after hooks.
- Sanic-style listener hooks, background tasks, and handlers.
- Full WSGI application mounting support.
- Built-in CORS, GZip, RequestID, RateLimit, BodyLimit, CSRF ASGI middleware.
- Session backends: secure cookies, in-memory, Redis.
- Pagination utilities for list endpoints.
- WebSocket per-route timeout support.
- Signal system for event-driven programming.
- Pluggable JSON serialization with tagged types.
- Auto-generated OpenAPI 3.0.3 schema with Swagger UI and ReDoc.
- Powered by high-speed Asteri ASGI server.
